summary: Phase 3 testing complete for 'Gipsy Word Connect'. Backend: 6/6 pytest cases pass (init=100 coins, ad_reward increments coins+ads_watched, word_reveal decrements coins, insufficient coins→400, unknown player→404, reset preserves name AND resets coins to 100 default). Frontend: Title='Gipsy Word Connect' (HTML+subtitle GIPSY WORD CONNECT visible), default language=Romanes (page title shows '100 ranga · Romane khelipena'), CoinsDisplay shows 100 on home AND inside game, 100 level tiles render, level-button-1 navigates to /level/1 with word-circle/crossword-board/word-preview (showing '—'), Lightbulb hint opens AdModal → after 5s skip → coins go 100→150 (verified), Hammer hint opens AdModal → skips successfully, Level 3 is anagram (anagram-slots + anagram-tiles), Level 6 is word_search (no word-circle, has hammer/lightbulb/buy-word + coins display). FAIL: Tap-to-tap letter selection on WordCircle does NOT accumulate — clicking circle-letter-N in sequence (D→A→J) leaves word-preview stuck at '—' because the container's onPointerDown handler resets selection to [idx] on each click and pointerup with len<2 clears it, then the `<g>` onClick fires after draggingRef is reset. This blocked Test 5 (level-1 solve) and Test 6 (congrats→ad→level-2 nav).

ui_bugs.issue: Tap-to-tap letter selection broken: clicking individual circle-letter-N elements does not accumulate selection. The parent container's onPointerDown sets selected=[idx] (REPLACES, not appends), and pointerup with selected.length<2 calls finish() which resets selected to []. The subsequent React onClick on the <g> then re-adds only the current letter via handleLetterTap. Net effect: each tap REPLACES the previous one and the word-preview never grows past 1 character. Users on desktop/click-only devices cannot solve word_connect levels via taps; only drag works. Verified empirically: preview text remained '—' after tapping D, A, J sequentially.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot test end-to-end because of WordCircle tap bug above. Backend completion endpoint and AdModal skip flow work in isolation (verified on lightbulb +50 coin test which uses identical AdModal mechanics).

issues: AudioContext warnings spam the console ('AudioContext was not allowed to start...') — MusicPlayer attempts to start audio before user gesture. Should defer creation/resume until first user interaction to avoid console noise and silent autoplay failure.

action_items: FIX WordCircle.jsx tap-to-tap: in handlePointerDown, don't immediately call setSelected([idx]) on press. Instead detect drag-vs-tap (e.g., only set selected on first pointermove, or only invoke finish() if draggingRef moved through >1 cell). Alternatively, separate the pointer-capture container from the per-letter click target so onClick on <g> can accumulate without container intercepting.
Resume/create AudioContext only after first user gesture to eliminate console warnings.

Consider exposing `data-testid='congrats-continue'` on the CongratsToast continue button (current code has the toast but the continue button selector wasn't found by query; main agent should verify the testid exists).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py reset_progress correctly preserves name (verified) — Phase 3 spec note answered: reset DOES rebuild PlayerProgress with default coins=100, ads_watched=0, completed_levels=[], current_level=1; name is preserved.

server.py update_coins: payload.delta < 0 check uses (prog.coins or 0) + delta < 0 which is correct; but the line before sets new_coins via max(0,...) — that max() is dead code because the check above already 404s on insufficient. Minor but harmless.

WordCircle.jsx mixing pointer events on parent with onClick on <g> children causes race conditions documented above. Either fully embrace drag (and remove handleLetterTap) or fully embrace tap (use only click handlers, no pointerdown selection mutation).
AdModal correctly uses 5s countdown and disables skip-button until remaining===0. The data-testid='ad-skip-button' becomes enabled at 0s and clicking it cleanly closes the modal.
Backend insufficient-coins guard returns 400 BEFORE write — verified via test (coins remain 100 after rejected -500 spend).

success_rate.frontend: 80% (8/10 critical flows passing; tap-to-tap blocks 2 dependent tests)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created TEST_InitCoins, TEST_Phase3Coins (multiple), TEST_ResetName in MongoDB. Existing phase1/phase2 seed users still present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 backend fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_phase3_coins.py (6 tests, all pass). Frontend testIds verified: page-title, coins-display, level-button-{1..100}, word-circle, crossword-board, word-preview, circle-letter-{0..N}, word-submit, hint-hammer, hint-lightbulb, buy-word, ad-modal, ad-skip-button, ad-countdown, congrats-toast, anagram-slots, anagram-tiles. To test post-solve flows you must either (a) fix WordCircle tap-to-tap, or (b) inject completion via fetch('/api/progress/complete') and navigate directly to /level/N+1, or (c) simulate drag with a sequence of pointer move events through the SVG (page.mouse.move/down/move/up). Use localStorage.setItem('romword_onboarded','1') + romword_lang='rmn' before page load to skip onboarding and force Romanes.
rca of the issue: WordCircle tap-bug RCA: handlePointerDown sets draggingRef=true and selected=[idx] on any pointer press. Single click (no movement) fires pointerdown→pointerup. In handlePointerUp (called via onPointerUp), draggingRef is true so finish() runs. finish() submits if selected.length>=2 (no — only 1) then setSelected([]) and draggingRef=false. AFTER pointerup, the React synthetic click event fires on the <g> child → handleLetterTap(idx) → since draggingRef now false, it adds idx, so selected=[idx]. Each subsequent click repeats the same: pointerdown overrides selected to [newIdx], pointerup clears, click adds [newIdx]. Result: selection never accumulates past length 1. Fix: in handlePointerDown, do NOT call setSelected([idx]) immediately — instead only mark draggingRef and wait until handlePointerMove detects a second letter to seed the drag. Alternatively, gate the pointerdown selection on `e.pointerType === 'touch'` or detect movement before initiating drag.
